{"step_by_step_thinking": "The tuberculin test can produce false-negative results in patients with compromised immunity, including those at the extremes of age, PLHIV, or prescribed immunosuppressive drugs. High percentage of immunized people, especially with BCG vaccination, can lead to false-positive results due to cross-reactivity. High prevalence of disease and environmental mycobacterium infections can complicate the interpretation of the test, but they are not specifically mentioned as leading to high failure in interpretation. Therefore, the situation with high percentage of immunized people is the most likely to cause high failure in the interpretation of the test.", "answer_choice": "A"}
